summary: Iteration 134: Modification Visibility & Bidirectional Features - All tests pass. Verified P0.1 (GET /api/modifications/mine returns all required fields), P0.2 (Dashboard shows 'Modifications en attente' section), P0.3 (Timeline cards show modification badges), P0.4 (Both organizer AND participant can respond to proposals), P1.1 (Participant can propose modifications from appointment detail), P1.2 (Participant can propose/respond/cancel via JWT). Non-regression tests confirm organizer flows still work.

backend_api_tests.test_02_modifications_mine_endpoint: PASS - GET /api/modifications/mine returns 200 with proposals array
backend_api_tests.test_03_modifications_mine_fields: PASS - Returns all required fields: proposal_id, appointment_id, appointment_title, start_datetime, proposed_by, status, mode, my_role, my_response_status, is_action_required, participants_summary
backend_api_tests.test_04_is_action_required_logic: PASS - is_action_required=true only when my_response_status=pending AND proposal status=pending
backend_api_tests.test_05_participant_propose_via_jwt: PASS - Participant can POST /api/modifications/ with JWT (no invitation_token needed)
backend_api_tests.test_06_organizer_respond_to_participant: PASS - Organizer can POST /api/modifications/{id}/respond to participant's proposal via JWT
backend_api_tests.test_07_participant_cancel_own_proposal: PASS - Participant can POST /api/modifications/{id}/cancel for their own proposal via JWT
backend_api_tests.test_08_organizer_direct_mode: PASS - Organizer modification returns mode='direct', status='auto_applied' when 0 accepted non-org participants
backend_api_tests.test_09_organizer_proposal_mode: PASS - Organizer modification returns status='pending' when >=1 accepted non-org participant

frontend_tests.dashboard_modifications_section: PASS - Participant dashboard shows 'Modifications en attente (1)' section with pending modification
frontend_tests.timeline_modification_badges: PASS - Timeline cards show 'Action requise' badge for appointments with pending modifications
frontend_tests.appointment_detail_proposal_banner: PASS - Appointment detail shows amber banner 'Modification en attente de votre réponse' with Accept/Reject buttons
frontend_tests.proposal_response_buttons: PASS - Accept and Reject buttons visible and functional for participant

P0.1_modifications_mine_endpoint.description: GET /api/modifications/mine returns all proposals involving current user with correct fields

P0.2_dashboard_modifications_section.description: Dashboard shows 'Modifications en attente' section when is_action_required=true

P0.3_timeline_modification_badges.description: Timeline cards show amber 'Action requise' badge or blue 'Modification en cours' badge

P0.4_bidirectional_respond.description: Both organizer AND participant can respond to proposals via JWT

P1.1_participant_propose_from_detail.description: Participant can open edit modal if viewerCanPropose=true (accepted status)

P1.2_participant_jwt_operations.description: Participant can propose/respond/cancel via JWT (not just invitation_token)

critical_code_review_comments: GET /api/modifications/mine correctly enriches proposals with my_role, my_response_status, is_action_required
JWT-based participant resolution in POST /api/modifications/ correctly identifies participant by user_id
Frontend ModificationProposals component correctly shows Accept/Reject for any user with pending response
Dashboard correctly filters pendingModifications.filter(p => p.is_action_required) for action section
context_for_next_testing_agent: Modification visibility features fully implemented and tested. Key endpoints: GET /api/modifications/mine (returns all proposals with enriched fields), POST /api/modifications/ (participant can propose via JWT), POST /api/modifications/{id}/respond (both roles can respond via JWT), POST /api/modifications/{id}/cancel (proposer can cancel via JWT). Frontend shows 'Modifications en attente' section on dashboard and amber banner with Accept/Reject on appointment detail. Note: Login API returns 'access_token' not 'token'.
